overview:
**Very High fire danger persists today, driven by critically dry fuels (ERC at 92%, exceeding local thresholds) and very low overnight humidity.**
- Danger eases to High from Tuesday onward as afternoon showers return later this week, per the 7-day forecast.
- Winds stay light (7-11 mph, NE), but dry fuels and low humidity (18-23%) keep afternoon fire growth likely.
- Watchout status: 2 of 7 local thresholds tripped today (ERC and min RH); no Red Flag Warning currently posted for this area.
- Montrose County remains under Stage 2 Fire Restrictions effective since Wednesday, July 1, 2026, due to extreme fire danger and ongoing wildfire activity across Colorado.
- Nearby: the Gold Mountain Fire near Ouray was estimated at 37,809 acres and 13% contained Saturday morning; the Elk Fire north of Lake City was estimated at 2,274 acres with no containment estimate available as of Saturday, after "somewhere between a quarter inch and a half-inch of rain" helped slow growth on both fires.
lead: Very High fire danger persists today, driven by critically dry fuels (ERC at 92%, exceeding local thresholds) and very low overnight humidity.

sources.name: Montrose County Implements Stage 2 Fire Restrictions Beginning July 1 • Montrose County
Aspen Acres fire surpasses 100,000 acres as crews increase containment to more than 60%
